reviews.review_title: It is a demo of a concept
reviews.review_description: I’d love for them to run with this VR app on chemistry. This app can be finished in like 10 minutes as it is in “early access” stage. I’m bummed by the length of it, but I’m sure we will eventually get a finished product in due time. VR is definitely the future of education and career training. It would be amazing if they did college level general chem 1 & 2 and organic chem 1 & 2. Organic chem in the US has a high failure rate, and a cool app like this could resolve a lot of those issues. Personally, I was the only person in my organic course to get 100% on exams and scored in the top 3% in the country in the standardized exit exam at the end of organic 2. Still, I love organic chemistry and would love to brush up on a lot of the things I learned in the course like synthesis reactions which stumped a lot of students as it demands full mastery of all the types of reactions learned throughout the course and even prior courses.
When was the last update, also any updates?

I really like this game but I just feel like they need to add more chemical compounds to it and also tell you, what specific bonds you have to use as well That was kind of annoying when I was playing it

Few suggestions for improvement from a fellow VR developer:
1. The instructions on how to build the first compound were very clear with the hand gestures guiding me. But they disappeared suddenly (probably some sort of bug) and I was left on my own from there, not sure what to do, I had to just randomly try different gestures to figure out there was a menu to choose the bond type.

2. The vortex for cleaning out the stuff is really cool. But sometimes when I put an atom into it, the bond menu opens up and I'm unable to close it. Even if I select a new atom and try to open its menu, it wont open.

3. Some sort of guidance of what ionic or covalent bonds are would be cool as I was just doing a trial and error method of making the connections. Learning about it in a deeper level might be helpful in creating these molecules.

4. Lastly was really stuck unity I accidentally figured out that I can convert the molecule into their real form (animation for it is fantastic). I was still confused as the circle in the bottom which fills up to show if the process is complete, doesn't fully fill up, even after completing the molecule (I may be wrong).
